from app.ai.conversation_memory import outbound_count
import pytest

def test_single_message_landlord_returns_zero():
    messages = [{"sender": "landlord", "message": "Can you share your WhatsApp number?"}]
    assert outbound_count(messages) == 0

def test_empty_list_returns_integer():
    messages = []
    result = outbound_count(messages)
    assert isinstance(result, int)

def test_multiple_messages_all_landlord_same_message():
    messages = [
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Can you share your WhatsApp number?"},
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Can you share your WhatsApp number?"}
    ]
    result = outbound_count(messages)
    assert isinstance(result, int)

def test_message_missing_sender_key_raises_exception():
    messages = [{"message": "Hello"}]
    with pytest.raises(KeyError):
        outbound_count(messages)

def test_message_missing_message_key_raises_exception():
    messages = [{"sender": "landlord"}]
    with pytest.raises(KeyError):
        outbound_count(messages)

def test_message_sender_not_string_raises_exception():
    messages = [{"sender": 123, "message": "Hello"}]
    with pytest.raises(TypeError):
        outbound_count(messages)

def test_message_message_not_string_raises_exception():
    messages = [{"sender": "landlord", "message": 456}]
    with pytest.raises(TypeError):
        outbound_count(messages)

import pytest
from app.ai.conversation_memory import phone_shared_state

def test_returns_false_when_landlord_asks_for_whatsapp_and_phone_fetching_type_delayed():
    messages = [{"sender": "landlord", "message": "Can you share your WhatsApp number?"}]
    persona = {
        "persona_name": "James",
        "persona_partner_name": "Sophie",
        "persona_job": "Software Engineer",
        "persona_partner_job": "Project Coordinator",
        "household_description": "professional couple",
        "message_tone": "friendly, direct, brief",
        "home_city": "Manchester",
        "mobile_number": "+447900111222",
        "phone_fetching_type": "delayed",
        "message_strategy": "friendly viewing first",
        "escalation_behavior": "wait until logistics are specific",
        "conversation_goal": "arrange a viewing and coordinate contact details"
    }
    conversation = None
    result = phone_shared_state(messages, persona, conversation)
    assert result is False

def test_returns_boolean_for_empty_messages_list():
    messages = []
    persona = {
        "persona_name": "Alice",
        "mobile_number": "1234567890",
        "phone_fetching_type": "delayed"
    }
    result = phone_shared_state(messages, persona)
    assert isinstance(result, bool)

def test_returns_boolean_when_conversation_is_none():
    messages = [{"sender": "landlord", "message": "Can you share your WhatsApp number?"}]
    persona = {
        "persona_name": "Bob",
        "mobile_number": "0987654321",
        "phone_fetching_type": "delayed"
    }
    result = phone_shared_state(messages, persona, conversation=None)
    assert isinstance(result, bool)

def test_returns_boolean_when_phone_fetching_type_is_not_delayed():
    messages = [{"sender": "landlord", "message": "Can you share your WhatsApp number?"}]
    persona = {
        "persona_name": "Carol",
        "mobile_number": "5555555555",
        "phone_fetching_type": "immediate"
    }
    result = phone_shared_state(messages, persona)
    assert isinstance(result, bool)

def test_returns_boolean_when_mobile_number_missing_in_persona():
    messages = [{"sender": "landlord", "message": "Can you share your WhatsApp number?"}]
    persona = {
        "persona_name": "Dave",
        "phone_fetching_type": "delayed"
    }
    result = phone_shared_state(messages, persona)
    assert isinstance(result, bool)

def test_returns_boolean_when_message_does_not_contain_keyword():
    messages = [{"sender": "landlord", "message": "Hello, how are you?"}]
    persona = {
        "persona_name": "Eve",
        "mobile_number": "4444444444",
        "phone_fetching_type": "delayed"
    }
    result = phone_shared_state(messages, persona)
    assert isinstance(result, bool)

from datetime import datetime
import pytest
from app.ai.stages import extract_viewing_datetime

def test_extract_viewing_datetime_tomorrow_with_pm_time():
    now = datetime(2026, 5, 19, 9, 0)
    messages = [{"sender": "landlord", "message": "I can do 5pm or 7pm tomorrow."}]
    result = extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now)
    expected = datetime(2026, 5, 20, 19, 0)
    assert result == expected

def test_extract_viewing_datetime_multiple_landlord_messages_last_confirmed_used():
    now = datetime(2026, 5, 19, 9, 0)
    messages = [
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Confirmed, see you tomorrow at 6pm."},
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Can we reschedule? Thursday at 8pm works."}
    ]
    result = extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now)
    expected = datetime(2026, 5, 21, 20, 0)  # Thursday after May 19, 2026 is May 21
    assert result == expected

def test_extract_viewing_datetime_time_without_am_pm_interpreted_as_afternoon():
    now = datetime(2026, 6, 4, 17, 0)
    messages = [{"sender": "landlord", "message": "Viewing booked for tomorrow at 3."}]
    result = extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now)
    expected = datetime(2026, 6, 5, 15, 0)
    assert result == expected

def test_extract_viewing_datetime_explicit_date_and_time():
    now = datetime(2026, 6, 4, 9, 0)
    messages = [{"sender": "landlord", "message": "Confirmed for 05/06 at 3."}]
    result = extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now)
    expected = datetime(2026, 6, 5, 15, 0)
    assert result == expected

def test_extract_viewing_datetime_case_insensitive_matching():
    now = datetime(2026, 5, 19, 9, 0)
    messages = [{"sender": "landlord", "message": "confirmed, see you TOMORROW at 6PM."}]
    result = extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now)
    expected = datetime(2026, 5, 20, 18, 0)
    assert result == expected

def test_extract_viewing_datetime_weekday_resolution_next_occurrence():
    now = datetime(2026, 5, 19, 9, 0)  # Tuesday
    messages = [{"sender": "landlord", "message": "Let's meet on Thursday at 8pm."}]
    result = extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now)
    expected = datetime(2026, 5, 21, 20, 0)  # Next Thursday after May 19 is May 21
    assert result == expected

import pytest
from app.ai.personas import get_conversation_style

def test_get_conversation_style_known_keys_return_expected_dicts():
    # landlord_number_boundary
    result = get_conversation_style("landlord_number_boundary")
    assert isinstance(result, dict)
    assert set(result.keys()) == {
        "label", "strategy", "phone_fetching_type", "escalation_behavior", "conversation_goal"
    }
    assert result["label"] == "Landlord number with boundaries"
    assert result["strategy"] == (
        "Avoid sharing the tenant number, keep OpenRent as fallback, and ask for the landlord number only when viewing logistics make it reasonable."
    )
    assert result["phone_fetching_type"] == "landlord_requests_only"
    assert result["escalation_behavior"] == (
        "respect refusals, show mild discomfort about sharing tenant contact details, and wait before re-asking"
    )
    assert result["conversation_goal"] == (
        "get the landlord's number without making contact capture feel like the main goal"
    )

    # friendly_viewing
    result = get_conversation_style("friendly_viewing")
    assert result["label"] == "Friendly viewing first"
    assert result["strategy"] == "Ask about viewing availability first, then coordinate contact details naturally."
    assert result["phone_fetching_type"] == "viewing_first"
    assert result["escalation_behavior"] == "warmly nudge toward a fixed viewing time before exchanging phone numbers"
    assert result["conversation_goal"] == "book a viewing and build enough trust for contact exchange"

    # professional_polite
    result = get_conversation_style("professional_polite")
    assert result["label"] == "Professional polite"
    assert result["strategy"] == "Keep replies measured, practical, and polite with minimal extra detail."
    assert result["phone_fetching_type"] == "adaptive"
    assert result["escalation_behavior"] == "adapt to the landlord's pace while keeping the thread practical"
    assert result["conversation_goal"] == "arrange a viewing and collect contact details when appropriate"

    # busy_professional
    result = get_conversation_style("busy_professional")
    assert result["label"] == "Busy professional"
    assert result["strategy"] == "Mention work schedules lightly and prefer phone coordination once timing is being discussed."
    assert result["phone_fetching_type"] == "adaptive"
    assert result["escalation_behavior"] == "ask for a direct contact route when schedules become specific"
    assert result["conversation_goal"] == "coordinate efficiently around work availability"

    # whatsapp_coordination
    result = get_conversation_style("whatsapp_coordination")
    assert result["label"] == "WhatsApp coordination"
    assert result["strategy"] == "Use WhatsApp as the preferred coordination channel in a casual but not forceful way."
    assert result["phone_fetching_type"] == "whatsapp_first"
    assert result["escalation_behavior"] == "share or request WhatsApp details early when it feels natural"
    assert result["conversation_goal"] == "move the conversation to WhatsApp for viewing coordination"

@pytest.mark.parametrize("invalid_style", [
    "",  # empty string
    "Landlord_number_boundary",  # case mismatch
    "friendly",  # substring only
    "professional_polite ",  # trailing space
    "busyprofessional",  # missing underscore
    "WHATSAPP_COORDINATION",  # uppercase
    "random_style",  # unknown string
    "landlord_number_boundary\n",  # newline appended
])
def test_get_conversation_style_invalid_keys_raise_exception(invalid_style):
    with pytest.raises(Exception):
        get_conversation_style(invalid_style)

def test_get_conversation_style_return_dict_keys_and_types():
    for style in [
        "landlord_number_boundary",
        "friendly_viewing",
        "professional_polite",
        "busy_professional",
        "whatsapp_coordination",
    ]:
        result = get_conversation_style(style)
        assert isinstance(result, dict)
        keys = {"label", "strategy", "phone_fetching_type", "escalation_behavior", "conversation_goal"}
        assert set(result.keys()) == keys
        for key in keys:
            assert isinstance(result[key], str)
        # phone_fetching_type must be one of known values
        assert result["phone_fetching_type"] in {
            "landlord_requests_only",
            "viewing_first",
            "adaptive",
            "whatsapp_first",
        }

def test_get_conversation_style_exact_match_required():
    # Partial match should fail
    with pytest.raises(Exception):
        get_conversation_style("landlord")
    with pytest.raises(Exception):
        get_conversation_style("friendly_view")
    # Case sensitive
    with pytest.raises(Exception):
        get_conversation_style("Friendly_viewing")
    # Leading/trailing whitespace
    with pytest.raises(Exception):
        get_conversation_style(" friendly_viewing ")
    # Correct exact match returns dict
    result = get_conversation_style("friendly_viewing")
    assert isinstance(result, dict)

def test_get_conversation_style_no_kwargs_allowed():
    # The spec says no keyword arguments used or recognized
    # Passing style as keyword arg should raise TypeError
    with pytest.raises(TypeError):
        get_conversation_style(style="friendly_viewing")

from app.ai.conversation_memory import outbound_count
import pytest

def test_single_message_landlord_returns_zero():
    messages = [{"sender": "landlord", "message": "Can you share your WhatsApp number?"}]
    assert outbound_count(messages) == 0

def test_empty_list_returns_integer():
    messages = []
    result = outbound_count(messages)
    assert isinstance(result, int)

def test_single_message_non_landlord_returns_integer():
    messages = [{"sender": "tenant", "message": "Hello, I am interested."}]
    result = outbound_count(messages)
    assert isinstance(result, int)

def test_multiple_messages_returns_integer():
    messages = [
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Can you share your WhatsApp number?"},
        {"sender": "tenant", "message": "Sure, here it is."}
    ]
    result = outbound_count(messages)
    assert isinstance(result, int)

def test_message_missing_sender_key_raises_key_error():
    messages = [{"message": "Hello"}]
    with pytest.raises(KeyError):
        outbound_count(messages)

def test_message_sender_not_string_raises_type_error():
    messages = [{"sender": 123, "message": "Hello"}]
    with pytest.raises(TypeError):
        outbound_count(messages)

from datetime import datetime
import pytest
from app.ai.stages import extract_viewing_datetime

def test_extract_tomorrow_with_pm_time():
    now = datetime(2026, 5, 19, 9, 0)
    messages = [{"sender": "landlord", "message": "I can do 5pm or 7pm tomorrow."}]
    result = extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now)
    expected = datetime(2026, 5, 20, 19, 0)
    assert result == expected

def test_extract_named_weekday_and_last_confirmed_message():
    now = datetime(2026, 5, 19, 9, 0)  # Tuesday
    messages = [
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Confirmed, see you tomorrow at 6pm."},
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Can we reschedule? Thursday at 8pm works."}
    ]
    # Thursday after May 19, 2026 is May 21, 2026
    expected = datetime(2026, 5, 21, 20, 0)
    result = extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now)
    assert result == expected

def test_extract_time_without_am_pm_interpreted_as_afternoon():
    now = datetime(2026, 6, 4, 17, 0)
    messages = [{"sender": "landlord", "message": "Viewing booked for tomorrow at 3."}]
    expected = datetime(2026, 6, 5, 15, 0)
    result = extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now)
    assert result == expected

def test_extract_explicit_date_day_month_format():
    now = datetime(2026, 6, 4, 9, 0)
    messages = [{"sender": "landlord", "message": "Confirmed for 05/06 at 3."}]
    expected = datetime(2026, 6, 5, 15, 0)
    result = extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now)
    assert result == expected

def test_case_insensitive_keyword_matching():
    now = datetime(2026, 6, 4, 9, 0)
    messages = [{"sender": "landlord", "message": "confirmed for TOMORROW at 3pm"}]
    expected = datetime(2026, 6, 5, 15, 0)
    result = extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now)
    assert result == expected

def test_multiple_messages_last_confirmed_takes_precedence():
    now = datetime(2026, 5, 19, 9, 0)
    messages = [
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Viewing booked for tomorrow at 5pm."},
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Confirmed for Thursday at 7pm."},
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Can we do Friday at 6pm instead?"}
    ]
    # Last confirmed or most relevant is Thursday at 7pm (May 21, 2026 19:00)
    expected = datetime(2026, 5, 21, 19, 0)
    result = extract_viewing_datetime(messages, now=now)
    assert result == expected

from app.ai.conversation_memory import landlord_messages
import pytest

def test_returns_only_landlord_messages():
    messages = [
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Hello"},
        {"sender": "tenant", "message": "Hi"},
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Are you available?"},
        {"sender": "agent", "message": "Please respond"},
    ]
    expected = [
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Hello"},
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Are you available?"},
    ]
    assert landlord_messages(messages) == expected

def test_sender_match_is_case_sensitive():
    messages = [
        {"sender": "Landlord", "message": "Hello"},  # capital L
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Hi"},
        {"sender": "LANDLORD", "message": "Hey"},
    ]
    expected = [
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Hi"},
    ]
    assert landlord_messages(messages) == expected

def test_order_of_messages_preserved():
    messages = [
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "First"},
        {"sender": "tenant", "message": "Ignore me"},
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Second"},
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Third"},
    ]
    expected = [
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "First"},
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Second"},
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Third"},
    ]
    assert landlord_messages(messages) == expected

def test_message_content_not_used_for_filtering():
    messages = [
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "tenant is late"},
        {"sender": "tenant", "message": "landlord is late"},
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Please respond"},
    ]
    expected = [
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "tenant is late"},
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": "Please respond"},
    ]
    assert landlord_messages(messages) == expected

def test_no_landlord_messages_returns_empty_list():
    messages = [
        {"sender": "tenant", "message": "Hello"},
        {"sender": "agent", "message": "Hi"},
    ]
    expected = []
    assert landlord_messages(messages) == expected

def test_empty_input_list_returns_empty_list():
    messages = []
    expected = []
    assert landlord_messages(messages) == expected
